name: Mobile Oversight — Phase 6.5: Capacity Foundation (Load Balancer)
outcome_goal: Praxis has a first-class capacity module — runtime/capacity/admission.py with can_launch(spec, context) -> CapacityDecision — that is called by every workflow launch site (dag_workflow action='run' + workflow_chain.advance_workflow_chains + Phase 7's future healer). The body today is reactive: it wraps workflow_classes.throttle_policy.concurrent_runs. The *shape* is SaaS-ready: every core workflow table carries tenant_id TEXT DEFAULT 'praxis' and workflow_runs carries resource_pool_id TEXT DEFAULT 'default'. Every admission/deferral is logged to launch_admission_decisions so the future predictive scheduler has training data from day one. Zero behavior change vs. today's reactive throttle. Zero cost to existing single-tenant operation. Full optionality for multi-tenant / resource-pool-aware scheduling later.
anti_requirements: Do NOT change the observable behavior of today's reactive throttle. concurrent_runs still gates at the same point; this just moves the gate into a named callable.

Do NOT add tenant isolation logic (fairness, quotas, partitioning). The tenant_id column is a sentinel — all queries still operate on the default 'praxis' tenant. Fairness logic belongs in a later phase when a second tenant exists.
Do NOT introduce a predictive scheduler. The capacity module is reactive today. Predictive logic is post-ship.

Do NOT read caffeinate state inside can_launch. Physical-machine state (mac_awake, CPU) is downstream of capacity, not an input. The caffeinate policy module reads capacity signals, not the other way around.

Do NOT widen writes outside Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capacity/, Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/, Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow_chain.py, Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py (one hook site), Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/, Code&DBs/Workflow/system_authority/workflow_migration_authority.json, and Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/_generated_workflow_migration_authority.py.
Do NOT break existing throttle semantics — any existing workflow_classes.throttle_policy='{"concurrent_runs":N}' must continue to gate at the same count after the refactor.
Do NOT surface launch_admission_decisions in the mobile PWA metrics in this phase. That's a Phase 5 amendment, not this phase.
notes: Grounding: debate_mobile_capacity_first_class_loadbalancer_20260421 — crossfire on treating capacity as a first-class load balancer for public stability and SaaS readiness. Surviving insights were: (1) capacity is a first-class *module* with a minimal *runtime* (reactive today, predictive later, callers don't change); (2) tenant_id as sentinel, not feature; (3) capacity decisions must be logged; (4) physical-machine state is downstream; (5) 'load balancer' framing forces pool abstraction; (6) provider rate-limit headroom is the under-counted axis (deferred to Phase 7+); (7) DB-as-queue beats filesystem-as-queue (deferred). This spec ships (1)-(4) and the pool-id sentinel. The rest is post-ship.

jobs.label: wave0_capacity_migrations
jobs.description: Write migrations 202 (tenant + resource_pool sentinels) and 203 (launch_admission_decisions table). Register both in workflow_migration_authority.json and regenerate the authority module.
jobs.outcome_goal: Two migrations apply cleanly against postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis. Core workflow tables carry tenant_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'praxis'; workflow_runs additionally carries resource_pool_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default'. A new launch_admission_decisions table exists with columns (decision_id PK, decided_at, spec_path, tenant_id, resource_pool_id, decision, reason, signals JSONB, workflow_class_id NULL, workflow_run_id NULL). Both migrations are registered in the generated authority module. A SELECT of the columns succeeds on every target table.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/202_tenant_and_pool_sentinels.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/203_launch_admission_decisions.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c 'SELECT tenant_id, resource_pool_id FROM workflow_runs LIMIT 0' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c 'SELECT tenant_id FROM workflow_chains LIMIT 0' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c 'SELECT tenant_id FROM workflow_triggers LIMIT 0' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d launch_admission_decisions' && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage._generated_workflow_migration_authority import CANONICAL_MANIFEST; assert "202_tenant_and_pool_sentinels.sql" in CANONICAL_MANIFEST and "203_launch_admission_decisions.sql" in CANONICAL_MANIFEST'

jobs.label: wave0_capacity_module
jobs.description: Create runtime/capacity/ package with types.py (CapacityDecision, CapacitySignals, CapacityContext) and admission.py (can_launch callable). Today's body wraps workflow_classes.throttle_policy.concurrent_runs. Every call emits a row in launch_admission_decisions.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime/capacity/admission.py exposes can_launch(conn, spec, context) -> CapacityDecision. Given a spec and a context (workflow_class_id, tenant_id, resource_pool_id, requested_by_kind/ref), it counts running workflow_runs for the class, compares to workflow_classes.throttle_policy->>'concurrent_runs', and returns admitted=True when under the cap (with reason 'throttle.headroom_available') or admitted=False when at the cap (reason 'throttle.concurrent_runs_saturated'). Each call inserts exactly one launch_admission_decisions row with the full signals dict. Unit tests in tests/unit/test_capacity_admission.py cover: (a) under-cap admits, (b) at-cap defers, (c) unknown class admits with reason 'no_class_policy', (d) tenant_id defaults to 'praxis' when absent, (e) every decision lands a ledger row, (f) the signals dict contains running_count, limit, workflow_class_id, tenant_id, resource_pool_id.
jobs.prompt:
Create a new package Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capacity/ with:

1. __init__.py — exports CapacityDecision, CapacityContext, CapacitySignals, can_launch.

2. types.py:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Literal, Mapping

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapacityContext:
    workflow_class_id: str | None
    tenant_id: str = 'praxis'
    resource_pool_id: str = 'default'
    requested_by_kind: str | None = None
    requested_by_ref: str | None = None
    workflow_run_id: str | None = None  # set after run is durable; None at gate time

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapacitySignals:
    running_count: int
    concurrent_runs_limit: int | None
    workflow_class_id: str | None
    tenant_id: str
    resource_pool_id: str
    extra: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def to_json(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            'running_count': self.running_count,
            'concurrent_runs_limit': self.concurrent_runs_limit,
            'workflow_class_id': self.workflow_class_id,
            'tenant_id': self.tenant_id,
            'resource_pool_id': self.resource_pool_id,
            **dict(self.extra),
        }

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapacityDecision:
    admitted: bool
    reason: str
    signals: CapacitySignals
    decision_id: str  # the row written to launch_admission_decisions
    decision_kind: Literal['admitted','deferred','rejected'] = 'admitted'
```

3. admission.py — the load balancer entry point:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
import uuid
from typing import Any
from .types import CapacityContext, CapacityDecision, CapacitySignals

def can_launch(conn: Any, *, context: CapacityContext) -> CapacityDecision:
    '''First-class capacity gate. Called by every workflow launch site.

    Today: reactive throttle wrapping workflow_classes.throttle_policy.concurrent_runs.
    Tomorrow: same signature, richer body (predictive, per-pool fairness, provider
    rate-limit headroom). Callers do not change.
    '''
    signals, limit, running_count = _gather_signals(conn, context)

    if limit is None:
        decision_kind = 'admitted'
        reason = 'no_class_policy'
    elif running_count < limit:
        decision_kind = 'admitted'
        reason = 'throttle.headroom_available'
    else:
        decision_kind = 'deferred'
        reason = 'throttle.concurrent_runs_saturated'

    decision_id = f'capdec_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:16]}'
    _log_decision(conn, decision_id=decision_id, context=context,
                  signals=signals, decision_kind=decision_kind, reason=reason)
    return CapacityDecision(
        admitted=(decision_kind == 'admitted'),
        reason=reason,
        signals=signals,
        decision_id=decision_id,
        decision_kind=decision_kind,
    )
```

`_gather_signals` queries:
  - workflow_classes row by context.workflow_class_id: extract throttle_policy->>'concurrent_runs' as int or None
  - SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workflow_runs WHERE workflow_class_id = $1 AND status IN ('queued','dispatching','running') (or whatever the active statuses are per workflow_runs CHECK constraint)
  - packs CapacitySignals(running_count, concurrent_runs_limit, ...)

`_log_decision` INSERTs one row into launch_admission_decisions with (decision_id, spec_path NULL at this abstraction level — caller may update, tenant_id, resource_pool_id, decision=decision_kind, reason, signals=signals.to_json(), workflow_class_id, workflow_run_id=context.workflow_run_id, requested_by_kind, requested_by_ref).

NOTE: spec_path is not always known at can_launch time. Accept context.spec_path as an optional field on CapacityContext, default empty string, and store empty when unknown — callers that know it (chain dispatch, MCP workflow run) will pass it.

4. Tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_capacity_admission.py using a real local Postgres fixture. Cases:
  - under-cap admits, inserts 'admitted' row
  - at-cap defers, inserts 'deferred' row
  - unknown class admits with 'no_class_policy', inserts row
  - default tenant/pool values when context omits them
  - signals.to_json carries running_count, limit, workflow_class_id, tenant_id, resource_pool_id
  - two consecutive calls produce two distinct decision_id values

5. The module should have zero import of caffeinate, psutil, or any machine-state reader. Pure DB query + ledger write.

jobs.label: wave0_capacity_wiring
jobs.description: Wire runtime.capacity.can_launch into the two launch sites: control_commands.submit_workflow_run (single spec) and workflow_chain._submit_wave_runs (chain waves). On deferral, log the decision and mark the run as queued-with-capacity-hold (no new status; existing 'queued' status is fine, but the capacity_decision_id is attached so observers know why it's idle).
jobs.outcome_goal: Every code path that creates a workflow_runs row (MCP run action, CLI workflow run, chain wave dispatch) calls runtime.capacity.can_launch before the INSERT. When admitted=True: run is created normally, with workflow_runs.capacity_decision_id FK to launch_admission_decisions.decision_id. When admitted=False: no workflow_runs row is created yet; the spec is left in the queue-state (for chains, the wave_run stays in 'pending'; for direct launches, the caller receives a structured response {deferred: true, decision_id, reason, signals}). Integration test proves that launching two workflows of the same class when concurrent_runs=1 admits the first and defers the second with a real launch_admission_decisions row for each.
jobs.prompt:
Wire the capacity gate into the two workflow-launch sites.

1. Add workflow_runs.capacity_decision_id column (migration 204_workflow_runs_capacity_ref.sql):

```sql
ALTER TABLE workflow_runs
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS capacity_decision_id TEXT NULL
  REFERENCES launch_admission_decisions(decision_id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS workflow_runs_capacity_decision_idx
  ON workflow_runs (capacity_decision_id)
  WHERE capacity_decision_id IS NOT NULL;
COMMENT ON COLUMN workflow_runs.capacity_decision_id IS 'FK to the launch_admission_decisions row that authorized this run. NULL for rows created before Phase 6.5.';
```

Register 204 in workflow_migration_authority.json + regenerate.

2. Runtime hook at control_commands.submit_workflow_run (find the exact function that inserts into workflow_runs — it is the one called by request_workflow_submit_command):

Before the INSERT, build a CapacityContext from the submission (workflow_class_id lookup: resolve from workflow_definitions.workflow_class_id; tenant_id='praxis' for now; resource_pool_id='default'; requested_by_kind/ref from the command row; spec_path from the command). Call runtime.capacity.can_launch(conn, context=context). If admitted=False: do NOT insert workflow_runs; return a structured response that the caller surface can render as 'deferred due to capacity'. If admitted=True: include capacity_decision_id in the INSERT column list.

3. Chain-wave hook at workflow_chain._submit_wave_runs (the function that inserts workflow_chain_wave_runs then dispatches to submit_workflow_run): per-run capacity check before each sub-submit. If deferred, leave the wave_run in 'pending' and log one launch_admission_decisions row per deferred run. The advance_workflow_chains tick will retry on the next pass.

4. Integration test at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_capacity_gate_wiring.py:
  - Seed one workflow_classes row with throttle_policy='{"concurrent_runs":1}'.
  - Seed one workflow_definitions row bound to that class.
  - Launch workflow A via request_workflow_submit_command → assert admitted, workflow_runs row exists with non-null capacity_decision_id.
  - Launch workflow B (same class) before A completes → assert deferred, NO new workflow_runs row, one new launch_admission_decisions row with decision='deferred'.
  - Mark A succeeded, re-submit B → admitted.

5. Document the contract at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capacity/README.md in ~30 lines: 'can_launch is the single gate. Every caller that creates a workflow_runs row goes through it. Today reactive; tomorrow predictive. Do not add a second gate.'
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/204_workflow_runs_capacity_ref.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c 'SELECT capacity_decision_id FROM workflow_runs LIMIT 0' && cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q 'Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_capacity_gate_wiring.py'
